cases.description: No argument: COLUMN() returns the column of the cell containing the formula itself. This case's formula lands in F1 by default, and F is the 6th column
cases.expected_note: F is column 6; COLUMN() with no anchor argument returns the formula's own column

cases.description: Explicit single-cell reference argument overrides the formula's own location
In non-array (scalar) context, COLUMN() of a multi-column range returns the column number of the FIRST column of the reference
Sanity check with a larger column number (Z is the 26th column)
